title: Stabilize production deployment and frontend-backend connectivity (frontend-only)

requirements.summary: Fix frontend-side root cause(s) contributing to production deployment failure and ensure build/deploy logs capture actionable diagnostics without exposing details to end users.
acceptanceCriteria: A production deployment completes successfully without errors.
The live (production) app reflects the latest draft changes after deployment.
A brief, developer-readable explanation of the original failure cause is captured in logs/output during the build/deploy process (not shown to end users).

file_operations.description: Harden production configuration resolution for the backend canister ID (validate presence/format early), add developer-readable console diagnostics for missing/misconfigured values during production builds, and ensure actor creation configuration is derived from production-safe environment sources. Use the core-infrastructure component’s backend connection configuration patterns where applicable; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wrap the routed app with a top-level runtime error boundary/fallback so unexpected initialization errors don’t surface as a generic blank screen in production, while still logging developer-readable details to the console (not user-facing). Use shadcn-ui primitives already present in the app for consistent styling; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure production build metadata and asset references (icons/favicons) are valid and do not cause build-time or runtime errors; confirm the static asset paths align with the build-template-react/initial-app conventions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Resolve production runtime connectivity issues by ensuring the frontend resolves the correct backend canister ID in production and shows a clear retryable error UI when actor creation or initial backend calls fail.
acceptanceCriteria: In production, the frontend can initialize an actor and successfully call backend methods (e.g., fetch history) without "Canister Id not resolved" errors under normal conditions.
If actor creation or the first backend call fails, the UI shows a clear English error state with a retry action (and does not crash/blank screen).
No changes are made to immutable hook files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; any needed error handling is implemented outside those immutable paths.

file_operations.description: Update actor creation configuration to reliably resolve the backend canister ID in production (and fail fast with a logged, developer-readable reason if it cannot). Add a safe, explicit failure mode for actor creation that upstream UI can detect (without editing immutable hooks). Use the core-infrastructure component’s backend connection configuration capability; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a non-immutable helper hook that inspects the React Query actor query state (including error) and exposes: actor readiness, actor creation error (if any), and a retry function that triggers refetch/invalidation—without modifying frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a reusable, English-only error UI component that explains the connection problem in plain language and provides a Retry action. Build it using shadcn-ui components (e.g., Card/Button) already used elsewhere; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Use the new diagnostics hook to detect actor-creation failure and render BackendConnectionErrorCard with Retry instead of silently showing an empty list. Also handle failed record fetches by displaying a clear error state with retry. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add graceful handling for missing backend connectivity: if actor creation/fetch fails, show BackendConnectionErrorCard with Retry rather than only "Record not found". Ensure the page never renders a blank screen on failure. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Improve first-call failure handling by surfacing backend call errors to the UI (instead of returning empty arrays when the actor is unavailable due to errors). Integrate with useActorDiagnostics for retry flows, without changing immutable hooks. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Ensure the live app’s core flows (save daily record, view in History, open Record Details, copy/export) remain functional after connectivity and deployment fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: On the live app, a user can save a daily record for a chosen Balance Date and see it in the History list.
Opening the saved record in Record Details works and displays the expected entries.
Copy/export actions on the live app function as before (no regressions).

file_operations.description: Integrate the connection diagnostics to (a) show a clear, retryable error state when saving cannot proceed due to actor creation/initial call failures, and (b) keep existing copy functionality unchanged. Ensure user-facing text remains English. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure History reliably reflects newly saved records after successful saves by adding an explicit refresh/retry affordance where appropriate and by not masking fetch failures as empty states. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Confirm Record Details continues to render entries correctly after any query/diagnostics changes, and ensure Export/Copy actions remain unchanged and functional. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
